rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical CoCl2-catalyzed dehydrogenative diarylation and [2+2+2] cycloaddition of alkenes with indoles via radical-polar crossover to give 1,1-bis(indolyl)alkanes and dihydroindolocarbazoles
electrochemical_role: anodic oxidation of indole to indolyl radical cation (A); cobalt-mediated alkene addition gives alkyl radical D which is further anodically oxidized to alkyl cation E; cathode reduces protons to H2

key_intermediates.name: benzylic alkyl radical with indolyl substituent (D)
benzylic alkyl cation with indolyl substituent (E)
indolyl cation intermediate F (after electrophilic alkylation of second indole)
dihydro intermediate G en route to carbazole product

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes indole 2a to a radical cation (A) which deprotonates to an indolyl C3-radical (B); Co catalyst delivers B across the alkene to give a benzylic radical (D), which is anodically oxidized to a benzylic cation (E); electrophilic Friedel-Crafts trapping by a second indole gives diarylation product 3, while intramolecular cyclization of the analogous intermediate F/G with further anodic oxidation furnishes the [2+2+2] carbazole 4; protons are reduced at the Pt cathode to H2.
rate_determining_step: electrophilic alkylation of indole by the benzylic cation in the final indole-trapping step (small KIE kH/kD = 1.27)

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: diarylation product only trace; terindolone 6aa isolated in 27%, consistent with indolyl radical cation formation and electrophilic capture rather than HAT

probe_reagents.name: 1-(1-cyclopropylvinyl)-4-methoxybenzene (1v)
probe_reagents.observation: ring-opened byproduct 4va (24%) formed alongside 3vaa (60%), indicating a carbon-centered radical intermediate

probe_reagents.name: 3-cyclopropyl-1-methylindole (2s)
probe_reagents.observation: no reaction and starting materials recovered; rules out direct generation of indolyl C-radical via cyclopropyl ring opening

probe_reagents.name: 2-cyclopropyl-1-methylindole (2t)
probe_reagents.observation: diarylation product 3att formed in 61% without ring-opening, supports electrophilic (cationic) trapping at indole

probe_reagents.name: 3-deutero-1-methyl-1H-indole (2a-1D)
probe_reagents.observation: small KIE kH/kD = 1.27, supports electrophilic aromatic substitution rather than HAT in final indole trapping

probe_reagents.name: 3-iodo-1-methyl-1H-indole (2u)
probe_reagents.observation: no reaction with TBAI or TBAPF6 electrolytes, indicating iodide oxidation pathway is not operative

probe_reagents.name: TBAI (nBu4NI)
probe_reagents.observation: iodide alone or I2 gave little effect; quaternary ammonium iodide salts crucial; constant-potential experiments show diarylation does not include oxidation of iodide

notes: Authors disfavor a HAT pathway (small KIE) and disfavor iodide-mediated oxidation (no effect of I-/I2 alone, constant-potential control); nBu4NI acts as supporting electrolyte/mediator-like additive lowering anode potential through NaOTf/nBu4NI mixed electrolyte rather than as iodine redox shuttle. Switching to graphite/graphite cell and stronger current diverts the diarylation intermediate F via intramolecular electrophilic alkylation (G) into the [2+2+2] dihydroindolocarbazole product 4.
